summary: Comprehensive backend regression suite for RISEDUAL Mission Control monorepo. 38/38 pytest tests passed against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — covering health, JWT auth (login/me/refresh/wrong-password/no-token), all /api/shared/* read endpoints (receipts, memory-labels, calibrators, feature-builders, artifacts, overview), /api/admin/flags, /api/admin/diagnostics, all three /api/runtime/{alpha,camaro,chevelle}/* endpoints, observation-mode flag verification, runtime filter validation (400 on invalid), per-runtime memory label values constrained to {safe, review, quarantine}, and Mongo namespace isolation verification (8 collections each return non-empty data through their dedicated APIs).
action_items: OPTIONAL HARDENING — auth.py uses cookie-based session (Secure+SameSite=None) AND falls back to Authorization: Bearer header; both work, but the inline docstring still says 'JWT auth (cookie-based)'. Update docstring to reflect dual mechanism.

OPTIONAL — CORS is allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False. If the frontend ever needs to use cookie auth cross-origin, this will need to switch to explicit origins + allow_credentials=True. Currently fine because the React UI is using Bearer tokens from localStorage per /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
OPTIONAL — login_attempts collection has no TTL index. Brute-force lockout records accumulate indefinitely. Consider a TTL index on `ts` (≥15 min) inside ensure_indexes().
OPTIONAL — JWT_SECRET is committed in /app/backend/.env. Acceptable for preview, but rotate before production.

NON-BLOCKING — /api/auth/refresh only reads refresh_token from cookie; clients using Bearer flow (per credentials doc) cannot refresh. If the React UI relies on refresh, accept the refresh token in JSON body or Authorization header too.
critical_code_review_comments: Doctrine integrity verified: each runtime route imports only its own namespace constant from namespaces.py — no cross-namespace reads. Good.

Observation-mode invariant verified end-to-end: every receipt returned by /api/shared/receipts has executed=false, observed=true; all enforce flags read from env and surface as false in /api/admin/flags.

/api/auth/refresh is cookie-only (line 157) but auth/login response advertises refresh_token in body — inconsistency: a Bearer-flow client cannot use the returned refresh_token without storing it as a cookie (which the server doesn't accept on the refresh endpoint anyway). Either remove refresh_token from login body or accept it from body/header on /refresh.
auth.py module docstring says 'JWT auth (cookie-based)' but get_current_user supports Authorization: Bearer fallback — update docstring.
All shared/admin/runtime endpoints correctly project out _id ({'_id': 0}) — no MongoDB ObjectId leakage. Good.
Idempotent seed in shared/seed.py uses count==0 guards per collection — safe to restart. seed_admin updates the existing admin password if env changes — matches playbook.
Brute-force lockout is implemented (5 fails / 15 min) but login_attempts has no TTL index → unbounded growth.
All endpoints under /api/shared/*, /api/admin/*, /api/runtime/* correctly require Bearer/cookie via get_current_user dependency — verified by 401 tests on every route group.
Pydantic input validation present on LoginRequest (EmailStr). Other endpoints are GET-only with Query validation — fine.
namespaces.py is the single source of truth for collection names — good DRY/centralization.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Verified that /app/backend/shared/seed.py auto-seeds 5 feature builders, 6 calibrators, 6 artifacts, 45 ADL receipts, 36 memory labels, 3 alpha decisions, 2 camaro shadow rows, 2 chevelle memory labels — all idempotent on boot.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green (38/38). All 8 namespaced collections verified populated through their respective APIs. Auth uses Bearer header (preferred) with cookie fallback. /api/auth/refresh currently only honors cookie — note this if future tests cover refresh flow with Bearer-only clients. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_risedual_backend.py is the regression suite to re-run after any change.
